message:
feat(core,abi): port wordexp variable expansion to core::stdlib::wordexp (bd-ev-epic)

Lifts ~85 lines of inline POSIX wordexp variable expansion out of
unistd_abi.rs into a pure-safe core::stdlib::wordexp module. The
std::env::var() FFI dependency is replaced with a closure parameter
so core stays pure-safe and free of `std::env`.

New core surface:
  pub enum ExpandError { UndefinedVariable(String) }
  pub fn expand_vars<F>(
      word: &str,
      undef_is_error: bool,
      lookup_env: F,
  ) -> Result<String, ExpandError>
  where F: Fn(&str) -> Option<String>;

Implementation note: funnels through a private dyn-trait variant
(expand_vars_dyn) so the recursive double-quote expansion doesn't
blow up generic monomorphization (Fn -> &Fn -> &&Fn -> ...).

Supported expansions (matching the previous abi 1:1):
  - backslash escape (\X -> literal X)
  - single-quoted ('...') — verbatim, no expansion
  - $VAR / ${VAR} — environment lookup via the supplied closure
  - double-quoted ("...") — recursively expanded inside

abi changes:
  - expand_vars becomes a 9-line shim that delegates to core,
    passing |name| std::env::var(name).ok() and the WRDE_UNDEF
    flag check, mapping ExpandError::UndefinedVariable -> WRDE_BADVAL.

Verified via rch (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc_bd-ev-2):
  - 20 new core::stdlib::wordexp tests pass
  - 173/173 abi lib tests pass
  - 3/3 wordexp integration tests pass (NOCMD/UNDEF/BADCHARS quote
    semantics characterization)
  - cargo clippy -p core -p abi -D warnings clean
  - cargo fmt --check clean

Closes bd-ev-epic.
